_derivation_note: Setup spec derived from three parent instruments. Each parameter documents its inheritance source.

smart_guitar_geometry.scale_source: Les Paul / Explorer (not Klein — Klein is 648mm)
smart_guitar_geometry.fretboard_radius_source: Les Paul / Explorer (not Klein — Klein is 254mm). Open item: confirm before neck build.
smart_guitar_geometry.neck_angle_source: Les Paul (4.0°). Explorer is 4.5° — slightly steeper. Smart Guitar uses LP angle.

neck_relief._deviation: None — same scale length, same truss rod geometry
neck_relief.note: Bolt-on join at ~19th fret. Truss rod influence ends at body join. Frets 20-24 are body-side and not truss-rod correctable. Same 628.65mm scale as LP — relief target identical.

action_at_12th_fret._deviation: None at 12th fret. Frets 23-24 have no LP reference — extrapolate from 22nd.
action_at_12th_fret.bridge_note: Headless fixed saddles. Action set by saddle height screws per string. Fine tuners are for intonation only.
action_at_12th_fret.frets_23_24_note: No factory reference for 23rd/24th fret clearance from any parent. Extrapolate: if 22nd fret is clear at LP spec, 23rd and 24th should follow the same string path. Check for choke during bend test.

nut_height._inherited_from: none — locking nut is a different device
nut_height.pre_lock_check: Before clamping: fret at 3rd, gap at 1st fret barely visible. After clamping: nut slot depth is irrelevant — strings are clamped above the nut.
nut_height.note: Locking nut height is set by the nut itself. If action in open position feels wrong after clamping, the issue is saddle height, not the nut.

pickup_height._inherited_from: gibson_les_paul current factory + adjustment for direct mount
pickup_height._deviation: Direct body mount sits naturally 0.5mm closer to strings than pickup-ring mount. Start 0.5mm lower than LP factory spec.

neck_pickup._lp_factory_reference: LP neck: 3/32" (2.38mm) both sides. Direct mount correction: +0.8mm on bass side.

bridge_pickup._lp_factory_reference: LP bridge: 1/16" (1.59mm) both sides. Added 0.4mm bass side for radius.

pickup_height.magnetic_pull_test: Fret 12th, pluck low E, watch tuner through decay. Pitch drifts flat = too close. Back off 0.5mm.
pickup_height.balance_check: Match neck and bridge output at unity gain through amp.

intonation.bridge: Headless saddles adjust fore/aft. Same principle as any saddle: sharp at 12th = lengthen string (move saddle back). Flat = shorten (move forward).
intonation.fine_tuner_note: Fine tuners give ±semitone range. Start fine tuners at mid-travel before setting intonation.

string_recommendations.loading_note: Route through headless bridge first. Lock nut last after stretch and tune cycle.

setup_sequence: 1. Install ball-end strings through headless bridge. Do not clamp nut yet.
2. Tune to pitch. Stretch strings thoroughly.
3. Neck relief: capo 1st, hold 19th, check 8th — target 0.010" (0.254mm).
4. Tune again after truss rod settle (30 min).
5. Saddle height per string — target action at 12th fret (5/64" low E, 3/64" high e).
6. Clamp locking nut. Retune with fine tuners.
7. Intonation: fret 12th vs harmonic — adjust saddle fore/aft.
8. Pickup heights: fret 24th, set neck then bridge.
10. Play test: every string every fret. Bend high e 1.5 steps from 6th to 24th.

open_items.fretboard_radius: Must be specified before neck build. Recommend 304.8mm (12") from LP/Explorer lineage. Klein is 254mm — compound 254-305mm is an option.
open_items.pickup_models: Not yet selected. Heights above are starting points. Each model has its own optimal range.
open_items.bridge_model: Headless bridge model not yet selected. Hipshot, ABM, Steinberger all have different saddle height ranges.
open_items.neck_angle_confirmation: 4.0° assumed (LP). See neck_angle_analysis for solver results.

solver_discrepancy.note: Solver geometry model differs from Gibson reality. Likely missing body angle or neck pocket depth factor. Solver values are RELATIVE, not absolute.

smart_guitar_delta.conclusion: Neck angle CANNOT be finalized until bridge model is selected. Headless bridges sit higher than TOM — expect angle STEEPER than Explorer 4.5 deg, not shallower.

neck_angle_analysis.action_required: Select headless bridge model (Hipshot/ABM/Steinberger). Measure actual saddle height above body. Re-run solver with correct bridge_height_mm.

source_derivation.neck_relief: Gibson Les Paul factory — David (Memphis final inspector). Same 628.65mm scale.
source_derivation.action: Gibson Les Paul factory spec. Same scale, same measurement convention.
source_derivation.nut_height: Derived — locking nut is not user-adjustable for height.
source_derivation.pickup_height: Gibson current factory (1/16" bridge, 3/32" neck) adjusted -0.5mm for direct mount absence of pickup ring.

series_note: This spec covers the Production variant (fixed headless). See tremolo_setup_spec block for Pro series.

tremolo_setup_spec._applies_to: Smart Guitar Pro — headless floating tremolo
tremolo_setup_spec._parent_reference: No parent instrument has a tremolo. Procedure derived from double-locking floating tremolo first principles and FR Original geometry (floyd_rose_tremolo.py).
tremolo_setup_spec.locking_point: bridge_saddle_blocks as primary lock. FR locking nut can be retrofitted at the nut position using a standard FR nut — this is a builder workaround, not an official product. No purpose-built headless FR locking nut exists. Alternative: Sophia Tremolo individual string locks (individual set screws, no pair-locking penalty). Strandberg uses yet another mechanism.
tremolo_setup_spec.fundamental_difference_from_fixed: A floating tremolo is a force-balanced system. String tension and spring tension oppose each other across the pivot. Every adjustment changes the balance: tuning a string changes spring ratio, changing action changes string angle, changing spring count changes everything. Setup is iterative, not linear.

equilibrium_condition.plain_english: String pull forward must equal spring pull rearward for the bridge plate to be parallel to the body.
equilibrium_condition.target_plate_angle: parallel to body top — visually level, not tilted fore or aft
equilibrium_condition.check_method: Sight along the body from the tail. Bridge plate should be flush with body surface or tilted not more than 1-2mm at the front edge.
equilibrium_condition.note: On a headless tremolo the pivot geometry differs from FR Original — verify plate angle spec with the specific bridge unit manufacturer.

setup_sequence.overview: Must iterate through steps 3-6 multiple times. Budget 2-3 full passes before the system settles.

steps.name: String installation
steps.detail: Route ball ends through bridge. Do NOT clamp saddle blocks yet. Tune to pitch with fine tuners at center of travel.

steps.name: Spring pre-balance
steps.detail: Adjust spring claw position (or spring count) until bridge plate is approximately parallel to body at pitch. This is a rough balance — it will shift as strings are locked and stretched.

steps.name: Neck relief
steps.detail: Capo 1st, hold 19th, check 8th. Target 0.010" (0.254mm). Same as Production. Truss rod adjustments do not affect spring balance.

steps.name: String stretch and retune
steps.detail: Stretch all strings thoroughly. Retune. Bridge plate will shift — readjust spring claw if plate has tilted more than 1mm.

steps.name: Clamp saddle blocks
steps.detail: Once pitch is stable and bridge is level, clamp each saddle block. Retune with fine tuners.

steps.name: Action
steps.detail: Set saddle heights. On a floating tremolo, the bridge height (post height or plate tilt) affects action on ALL strings simultaneously — use this for coarse action. Individual saddle height for per-string fine adjustment.
steps.note: Same action targets as Production and Les Paul factory. Changing action may shift bridge angle — recheck plate level after.

steps.name: Rebalance springs
steps.detail: After action is set, bridge plate angle may have changed. Re-level the plate by adjusting spring claw. Retune.

steps.name: Intonation
steps.detail: 12th fret harmonic vs fretted. Adjust saddle fore/aft. Each saddle move changes break angle slightly — retune and recheck plate level if multiple saddles moved.

steps.name: Pickup height
steps.detail: Fret 24th. Same targets as Production — neck pickup low E 3.2mm, high e 2.4mm; bridge pickup low E 2.0mm, high e 1.6mm.

steps.name: Tremolo range test
steps.detail: Depress bar fully — all strings should return to pitch within 2 cents. Pull up if bridge allows — same check. If any string goes sharp on down-pull, saddle block may be slipping.

steps.name: Final play test
steps.detail: Every string every fret. Bends 6th to 24th. Use the tremolo arm through its range and verify return to pitch.

spring_configuration.note: Spring count and gauge affect both return force and feel. Bridge model determines spring type.

general_principle.more_springs_or_stiffer: Heavier string feel, less pitch drop per unit arm travel, more stable tuning
general_principle.fewer_springs_or_lighter: Lighter arm feel, more pitch range, more sensitive to string tension changes

claw_position.forward_toward_bridge: Less spring tension — bridge tilts forward (toward neck)
claw_position.rearward_away_from_bridge: More spring tension — bridge tilts rearward (toward tail)
claw_position.target: Position where bridge plate is parallel to body at pitch

tuning_stability_factors.string_stretch: New strings will detune for days. Stretch aggressively before clamping blocks.
tuning_stability_factors.saddle_block_grip: Headless bridge clamps strings at the bridge end. If a string slips in its block, it will go flat after tremolo use. Re-clamp.
tuning_stability_factors.pivot_friction: Knife edges or pivot screws must be smooth. Rough pivot = strings don't return to pitch. Polish or replace.
tuning_stability_factors.nut_note: No OEM headless locking nut exists. Retrofit options work but have limitations — shared-block FR nuts require loosening a neighbor string to change one string. Sophia Headpeace avoids this with individual locks. Whichever option: clamp after stretching and achieving pitch stability, then use bridge fine tuners for all subsequent tuning.
tuning_stability_factors.headless_locking_nut: If FR Original is used on Pro series: headless locking nut clamps strings at nut position. Same clamping function as a standard FR locking nut — no headstock required behind it. Once clamped, fine tuners at bridge handle pitch adjustment. Setup procedure is identical to a standard FR setup from the nut clamp step onward.
tuning_stability_factors.nut_retrofit_note: If retrofitting a standard FR locking nut for headless use: nut clamps strings in pairs. Breaking one string requires loosening its pair-neighbor to replace it. Confirmed working by builders (community reports 5+ years reliable use). Sophia headpeace individual locks avoid this penalty but are a premium specialty item.

blocked_tremolo_option.description: Pro series bridge can be blocked (tremolo disabled) for fixed-bridge feel on stage.
blocked_tremolo_option.method: Insert a hardwood or aluminum block in the spring cavity between claw and body wall, or use a Tremol-No style device.
blocked_tremolo_option.setup_when_blocked: Follow Production setup sequence — system behaves as fixed bridge when blocked.

open_items.bridge_model: Not yet selected. Spring type, cavity dimensions, pivot geometry, and saddle height range all depend on specific model.
open_items.spring_cavity_geometry: Cannot be finalized until bridge model is selected. Steinberger S-Trem vs Kahler vs Hipshot Trilogy all have different spring layouts.
open_items.floyd_rose_compatibility: Floyd Rose Original IS compatible with the Pro series. Requires a headless-compatible locking nut (Steinberger or aftermarket) at the nut position. No headstock needed. FR routing specs: floyd_rose_tremolo.py. Stud spacing 74.3mm, M7x0.5 inserts, standard (42mm) or medium (37mm) body routing depth.
open_items.headless_locking_nut: No official OEM headless locking nut. Best current option: Sophia Headpeace (individual per-string, purpose-built). FR retrofit works with limitations. Decision deferred pending bridge model selection.
open_items.floyd_rose_compatibility_note: Floyd Rose can be fitted to Pro headless variant via standard FR nut retrofit. No official headless FR locking nut product exists — this is a builder retrofit. Evaluate Sophia Tremolo individual string locks as a cleaner alternative. FR routing specs in floyd_rose_tremolo.py apply regardless of nut solution.

signal_chain_canonical.path_a: P90 → buffered splitter → 1/4 dry jack — 0ms, analog bypass
signal_chain_canonical.path_b: P90 → buffered splitter → Hi-Z USB → Pi 5 → HiFiBerry → headphone + WiFi
signal_chain_canonical.wifi: PipeWire network sink — Pi 5 built-in WiFi — no additional hardware
signal_chain_canonical.arduino: control only — footswitches, BMS, LEDs, relay bypass
